mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero and CPIAUCSL inflation subdued, the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally low, supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as both series stay within their current low-and-stable bands.
regime_hint: any regime with near-zero policy rate and low inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC between low-FEDFUNDS/low-CPIAUCSL regime indicator and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return frequency in this regime is not statistically greater than the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 5.5% and continues declining, markets price in an accelerated timeline for Fed liftoff, raising near-term discount-rate uncertainty and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as repricing of rate-hike odds compresses valuations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.5% and declines >=0.1pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% as of 2015-05 vintage nearing pre-crisis full-employment levels
failure_condition: In periods where the UNRATE decline trigger fires, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or rank IC is indistinguishable from zero (|IC|<0.02) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of decelerating INDPRO alongside continued PAYEMS employment growth signals a productivity-cost squeeze in the industrial sector that has not yet been reflected in labor markets, foreshadowing margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings guidance catches down to weak output data.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns negative while PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth remains positive
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 105.10 vs later revised 100.89 indicating overstated industrial strength in real time
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle divergence between goods-producing and services/labor sectors
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the INDPRO/PAYEMS divergence trigger are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to rank IC is not significantly greater than either single-series model (bootstrap p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
regime_hint: any regime with positive consumer spending momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or hit rate of positive returns following positive RSAFS growth does not exceed the unconditional base rate at 5-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero policy rate regime, rising HOUST housing starts indicate that ultra-low mortgage rates are successfully stimulating rate-sensitive demand, a leading indicator of broader economic momentum that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this relationship weakens or reverses once policy normalizes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015-05 with HOUST at 1135-1190k units
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, rising HOUST fails to produce forward 60-day returns significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or the relationship's sign flips once FEDFUNDS exceeds 1% without a corresponding narrative update.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS begins rising from its near-zero base after an extended hold, the initial liftoff signal triggers a short-term risk-off repricing in equities as markets front-run tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.10pp from a base below 0.25% after at least 6 months of stability
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 0.07-0.12% through 2015 with market anticipation of 2015 liftoff
regime_hint: transition from zero-lower-bound to initial monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: Following a FEDFUNDS liftoff trigger event, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from a placebo random-date sample (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong PAYEMS job creation and a declining UNRATE jointly signal the economy nearing full employment capacity, which raises wage-inflation and Fed tightening expectations concurrently, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month that a single labor indicator alone would not capture.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141,679k with UNRATE at 5.5% in mid-2015 signaling tightening labor slack
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-growth/UNRATE-decline signal fails to produce a rank IC on forward 20-day returns exceeding that of either individual proxy by a statistically significant margin (bootstrap p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a regime of dollar strength and commodity price weakness, a decline in INDPRO signals eroding export competitiveness and manufacturing profitability that has not yet been priced into equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings revisions catch down.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 105.10 (2015-05) vs revised 100.89, indicating manufacturing softness amid 2014-2015 dollar strength and oil price collapse
failure_condition: In periods with INDPRO YoY growth below 1% during dollar-strength regimes, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or rank IC is not significantly different from zero (|IC|<0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.
